narrative.travel_description: I travelled using Obs 1 probably on public transport, maybe an Uber. Hung out in the shadows around the area maybe an hour or two before start. There was the usual Ruse of a VVIP pickup with security detail, earpieces, the whole bit! I had some minor celebrities in evening gowns manipulated/encouraged to participate in the entourage. I entered the vehicle as it arrived at Court (obs2). Visibly walked from limo to doorway/elevator/bouncer. Limousine departs with celebs in tow for a night at the Star Casino once I am out.
narrative.game_recount:
- tried to meet all the new players (1st time) so really just engaged in character with all players that would have the chat in character. There was the odd notable blanking and rudeness (presumably IC for "lore reasons"). No powers or spending influence.

- Premier finally addressed Carthians after keeping us waiting and proceeded to: set rules; offer explanations as to why this experiment needed to be done in a particular way; copped the verbal bluster of various Carthians trying to state covenant parity; stated a plan to restrict Elders and Blood Potency city-wide because "YOUNG" and "EXPERIMENT". Yusuf offered support to Carthian principles. tsk.

- Met with the other Carthians who seemed more concerned with establishing the rules for the committee to police each others behaviour according to a new rubric. One carefully designed so that all roles - EXCEPT PREMIER FOR SOME REASON - are shared like a fucking conch shell, so that no one can claim Regency except as proxy for the Movement. lol

- Noticed duplicitous dealings involving Invictus but had the Premier rudely "send me away"

- Made a ploy for Regency of Harbour against Reed to test the level of support. I did not manage to get a meeting with the Premier in time, or they forgot, whatever. My name was likely not even on the ballot. Just as well - I never found the voting box to place my influence. haha. OOC n00b.
narrative.standout_rp: Ludika - young Invictus (mekhet I think, maybe Ventrue - OOC I could NOT read any tags or sheets in the game unfortunately vision impairment). We had a fascinating IC conversation. She had real questions... Conrad Sonderguard. His innocent probing and gentle banter. Get close to that guy. Sneakiest bastard in court I reckon. Great IC interactions.

Ý̵̧̭Ù̴͓̐́S̵̟͖̐̑Ù̴͑́͝F̴͌̾̄͠
narrative.most_trusted_pc: `Einar TechnoViking` - perhaps. He seems ready to stand up to the Premier when they cross the line and betray Carthian principles but a little slow to see that line has already been crossed.
narrative.actively_harming_pc: Rene StD (Invictus), the real seat of power and influence in Sydney. THe worm in the Premiers ear. I have a verbal truce to work with, rather than against, Reed Justice (Conan) and I am happy to be true to that arrangement. But....Rene..... He's Invictus "Team Captian", Primogen, Regent of Docks, presumably way up in his clan status too. this guy has peak influence, more city status than the Premier probably.... Why has no one noticed? Am I living in LALA land?
narrative.aspirations:
Yusuf feels isolated and unsupported. The Mammon Cult (MMN) is in formative stage, but I dont really have any surety to bother building it up just for someone else to take. At the same time I have not been subtle with its presence. The cult served its initial purpose - the contrivance to get me to Sydney. It stands out as a target. Perhaps I will set it up at this stage as a honey-pot to lure investigation and actions of other kindred. I see the cult as a presently useful expendable asset.

I am guest everywhere, marginalised even in the Covenant. Yusuf has a job here in Sydney - to protect Brandy. But he sees both her and Eve witless puppets on a string of more organised powers. A dangerous predicament. Yusuf must help himself first. Hence taking OBS5. And working on his lair. Mt plans are to get safe and embedded. This court seems like a dangerours and specially curated fishbowl designed to fail. I worry about the consequences for the Carthian Movement. I fear we have been trojan horsed by Eve with a plan to humiliate the movement on the global stage as the "Carthian Experiment" fails spectacularly.

It is unlikely building clan status within the Carthians will be viable. They are riven, misguided and working against their own interests. Eve has already cleverly seen to that. Handing out boons to those who are giddy with the proximity to praxis and ignoring those not actively bending knee. The younger Carthians in the movement seem enthusiastic to embrace a "lets see hoe THEY like it attitude, rather than a lets do this properly".

The City status game is meta-unstable and already stitched up. There are inner and outer cliques quickly forming and Yusuf sees no way into the inner cliques. So Yusuf must build.... 
with what he has left in the space left to him. THE SEWERS.

The sewers and catacombs in the territory no one bothered wanting... with his Clan - the Nosferatu. There are not many Nos, and they don't usually play well - not with others and not with themselves. I will make a place in the catacombs. I will open it to others of my kind. I will see who will come to the Necropolis once it is built. The first bricks are laid in this downtime.

SHORT: GET MYSELF SAFE
MEDIUM: BUILD THE NOS, PROTECT MMN
LONG: NECROPOLIS UNDER THEIR FEET

feeding.method:
Presence 4 + Empathy 3 + Spec: Motives 1 = 12
Type: HUMAN blood
Style: I like the homeless people. The cold, dark and rains drive them into the corners where this crawler creeps. Face-in-the-crowd Yusuf blends in. He likes the dirt and the smell. 
He likes the taste, a goon-bag of wine and a blanket are gifts enough. They sit and talk about their shit lives. Yusufs life was probably shitter, but at least they feel it, Yusuf thinks. And he tastes the goon-bag and hardship in them equally. He drinks at the end of the bin-fire in the hours just before dawn - never killing these.... no never killing. This is an act of sharing in my mind... lol

*Normally* he eats from his HERD of Bankers and the Elites from behind the Mammon throne. Expensive cologne, pressed shirts. They offer their necks up - ecstatic and trembling.

It is a bland feast and sometime you just need "home*less-cooking".

sphere_actions.description: The Mammon cult may not be good for elite members, but on their climb up its ranks the entropy of their climb maketh the garden more verdant for all kindred...

sphere_actions.action_type: Ambience Change (Increase): Make a Territory delicious 😄
sphere_actions.description: The subtle pro-kindred enshittification of Sydney Harbour after dark. (For the Nos 3am vibe baby!!!)The territory will be both better (for all) and worse (for mortals) in a clever way. Events will simultaneously draw people into the Harbour district, but stupid track-works close down public transport just as events let out forcing walks home. Public works for safety that actually break existing lighting. Energy saving initatives means that there is dim-lights but far apart. cost savings. it is almost like the bad ideas are deliberate....

sphere_actions.action_type: Acquisition: Use your standing in Status/Mystery Cult to procure an item 💵
sphere_actions.desired_outcome: Reserve/Block access to chunks of sewer/rail networks
sphere_actions.description: The network of influence in Politics and Finance that is the MMN cult reaps its rewards in greased palms. The right bribe, the right contract, the right environmental report. Volia - whole chunks of subterranean tunnels sealed off with special works here - accidental concrete spill there, sealed off for the M11 tunnel. Tunnels, mostly bare, ugly, damp, infested. But directly underneath and nowhere all at once! It is a patchwork of forgotten RTA service tunnels, WW2 shuttered ammunition tunnels, redundant storm-water catchment sumps, etc... It is not ownership per se. It is sealing the entry points off little clusters and burying them administratively so they are nobody's business.

meta.st_notes:
yes.The surveillance cameras I am trying to procure are a gift for Conrad Sunderland. I will pass them on with the full chain-of-custody documentation that certifies they are not manipulated or modified in any way between the Israeli manufacturer and his secured delivery. It is up to his discretion whether to trust that - but I have not manipulated the cameras in any way. I make it very clear that these cameras are a gift, without strings or obligation attached and he is free to use them however he likes but he must note that there are 2 monitor portals for the cameras (there are 3 cams) and I have the second one. In the spirit of "a pleasure shared is a pleasure doubled". I will contact the player IC on Discord with my "attempt". I guess rolls will determine if it came off to secure them...

My nights are focused with either managing the cult of Mammons activities, which I see like a job taking several half nights every week. This occurs in the Shore. One of those, I will stop by the Crone Temple on night of service - explaining that the Mother offered invite that I have spontaneously accepted. (all true except the spontaneous part). This attendance is lip service to my Hostesses in the Shore. My initial actual curiosity in the sermon is minimal. But I have never actually witnessed any REAL CRUAC up close before and it might just be a surprise awakening. I should let Mother Crone Anichka roll against my Resolve+BP probably to see how compelling her sermon is. ;)

I like to inhabit the Harbour now. I like it neglected to be honest and I want to savour the shittyness before the kindred money moves in. I supplemnent herd feeding there. I lurk and menace. I want the sewers (only) to be a place of twisted vibes. particularly the near the manholes, drains and rail maintenance tunnels adjacent to my Safe Place. I want everything to avoid those corners except the cockies.

This serves the long-term goal to establish a SafePlace/Warren/Necropolis for the Nos.
[!!!] Im not going for any mechanical benefit from Nos rulebook. My XP is pre-spent in my head for the next 2 years... lol. At most I am trying to build up my own Clan status, by embodying peak-Nos'ness, supporting other clanspeeps, and reaching out along Nos lines so that the other Nos know the I/WE ARE here, lurking too. I'd be cool if we share SafePlace merits together, and my OBS5 Oubliette can make for a truly BIZONKERS commons. I reckon its narratively cool and want to try to get traction next game... I hope the STs agree?

feeding_review.description:
I like the homeless people. The cold, dark and rains drive them into the corners where this crawler creeps. Face-in-the-crowd Yusuf blends in. He likes the dirt and the smell. 
He likes the taste, a goon-bag of wine and a blanket are gifts enough. They sit and talk about their shit lives. Yusufs life was probably shitter, but at least they feel it, Yusuf thinks. And he tastes the goon-bag and hardship in them equally. He drinks at the end of the bin-fire in the hours just before dawn - never killing these.... no never killing. This is an act of sharing in my mind... lol

*Normally* he eats from his HERD of Bankers and the Elites from behind the Mammon throne. Expensive cologne, pressed shirts. They offer their necks up - ecstatic and trembling.

It is a bland feast and sometime you just need "home*less-cooking".

projects_resolved.st_response:
You go into the tunnels wearing a high-vis vest and a hardhat and come out wearing neither. In between, you walk every accessible passage beneath the Harbour district: storm drains, disused rail maintenance corridors, cable runs dating back to the 1930s that no longer appear on any current map. The Cave Clan have been using a junction chamber beneath Hickson Road as a base camp for years. By the end of your first night down there, they have abandoned it so completely that they leave behind two camping chairs and a battery lantern still switched on.

It is not subtle work. You move through the tunnels over the course of a week and everything that lives down there, human or otherwise, decides independently and with considerable urgency to be somewhere else. A Reddit thread appears in r/sydney titled "anyone else getting weird vibes under Barangaroo?" with fourteen comments and no coherent answers. Council receives three separate requests for emergency works on storm drain access points, all citing safety concerns that the callers struggle to articulate. The paperwork moves quickly. Grates get welded. Access hatches get new locks. The tunnels empty out, and what fills the silence afterwards is the particular stillness of a space that now belongs to something the previous occupants could smell but never quite see.

notes_thread.text: Safe Place now in Under Harbour
Its purring like a corrupt hat full of fucking deplorable arseholes. Tasty arseholes.
More finance bros out late networking in bars after working the grind over time
Late night trading is the talk of the town as the local members start loosening the grip on the nanny state.
Work on a Necropolis has begun.

merit_actions_resolved.st_response: Your enquiry into the Finance sphere returns a picture that confirms what you suspected. The serious Kindred players in this space are you, Xavier, and Reed Justice. Three hands, and three hands only, operating at the level that matters. What catches your attention is a thread at the edges of the report. Somebody has been paying for information in the Finance sector across the month, at a Resources rating consistent with sustained Contact work. The description of the buyer is consistent with Keeper. He has not positioned himself as a Finance player. He has positioned himself as a man who wants to know what the Finance players are doing. File accordingly.

Somebody calling herself Rene, a Carthian firebrand by reputation, has been throwing influence around in ways calculated to make the Harbour a little worse for wear. Small drags against the larger lift, targeted in places that suggest she knows what she is doing. She is working against you and Reed and Wan and everyone else who has been lifting the territory. The amount of influence is not enormous. The deliberateness of it is.

st_review.outcome_text:
## Letter from Home

Y,
[E]ndless [Y]arns from th[E] [S]outh, I'll spare y[O]u.
[N]othing new he[RE] worth the postage.
[N]ew faces [E]verywhere, same old arguments.
[HOLD] the line. Strange as ever.

## Touchstone

The Turkish laundrette woman is folding sheets at the big table near the back when you come in, and she does not look up because she does not look up for anyone; the door chime is for her ears alone and she has long since stopped turning her head at it. You stand by the dryers pretending to wait for a load. You have stood here before. The fluorescent tube above the counter has been buzzing on the edge of failure for at least eight months. She has not replaced it because the light is still light.
She works the way she has always worked. A sheet lifted, shaken out, aligned corner to corner, halved, halved again, smoothed down the middle, stacked. The stack grows at her left hand. She does not hum. Her hands are older than you remember but they are the same hands, slow and exact, and the pink scar on the base of her thumb is still there from the burn she got on the steam press the summer before you left the district.
A student comes in and asks about the machines, and she points at the sign without speaking, and he nods and goes to the machines, and she folds another sheet. You buy a small sachet of powder from the wall dispenser you do not need and leave coins on the counter. She does not count them. She never has. The door chimes behind you.

## The Harbour

The Harbour is warming and the corners you crawl into are warming with it. The rough sleepers under the Anzac Bridge approaches and in the service recesses at Barangaroo are more numerous this month, the cold bringing them deeper into the places you work. Face in the crowd, you take what you need and leave a bottle and a blanket, and the blood comes up full, clean, the territory giving back. Reed Justice passes you on the Pyrmont footbridge and looks past you, which is the only acknowledgement that matters. What everyone is talking about is a clip on the nightly news of a carpark near the Pyrmont overpass. On two separate streets past midnight the regulars you know by sight are not where they usually sleep, bad dreams moving them along.

## The North Shore

The Shore keeps its poor out of sight better than most territories, but the corners still hold. The rough sleepers behind St Leonards station, the ones in the loading docks at Artarmon, the ones nobody on Military Road admits are living two streets away. Face in the crowd, you take what you need and the blood comes up clean, a little shaded, a territory worked fairly. You pass Ivana Horvat near the North Sydney oval twice in the same week, both of you keeping to the fringes. At the back of an all-night pharmacy in Crows Nest a woman sees you before you have slipped, which is the month telling you someone has been paying attention.

## Very Very Sneaky

You have been building Obfuscate into yourself across the whole cycle and by the end of the month the final layer settles. Oubliette goes onto the Siphon the night after you seal the lair. The space edits itself out of the under-Harbour's mental map the way a typographical error edits itself out of a document once the proofread is done. The tunnels approach it and do not arrive. The rats go around. The maintenance engineers who know this part of the system have already begun to misremember whether the chamber was ever there. You sleep better than you have slept in a century.

## A fear cleanse of the Burrows

You walk the tunnels for three weeks and they give themselves up to you section by section. Maintenance overalls and a clipboard get you past the first two checkpoints. After that it is just you and Face in the Crowd and the dark, and the dark carries your work further than the work carries itself. The Cave Clan abandon their usual pitches in the first week. By the second the deeper settlements, the ones that have persisted since the Olympics, break up and move. You drift through the sleeping corners and let Nightmare do what Nightmare does. Grown men wake with their teeth chattering and run without being able to name what chased them. A woman curled beside a pipe dreams her way out into the street above before she is fully awake. By month's end the tunnels under Pyrmont, Balmain East, and the long loop beneath the Barangaroo foreshore and Hickson Road have emptied. The Burrows are yours to inhabit. Above ground the rough sleeper count has visibly risen, fresh bodies around the Anzac Bridge approaches and under the overpasses where there were none the week before. That is somebody else's problem. What you have built down here is a new kind of territory, unlit, unclaimed by any Regent, and waiting.

## A rotten Nest

The Siphon is a large chamber and it is mostly empty when you start. You spend the first week moving your possessions down through the service tunnels, the coffin, the ledgers, the pieces that matter. The second week is the vitae work. You shed blood into the corners and the blood holds the shape you want it to hold; the space begins to exhale wrongness. You engineer the approaches so that anyone who comes down the wrong corridor hears distant screaming before they hear footsteps, and the screaming is not yours. By the third week the Burrows around your new lair behave themselves without you having to patrol them; the space has acquired a reputation the rats pass on to each other. By the end of the month you sleep here. Safe Place now in the Under-Harbour.

## "MMN Sees"

The apparatus hums across the month. Networks maintained, new members grooming, resources allocated, the occasional small fire put out before it could become a large one. The North Shore arm in particular is in the best shape it has been in. A corrupt hat full of deplorable arseholes. Tasty arseholes. Xavier's arrival mid-cycle has tightened what you had already built in ways you might have resented from anyone else, and from Xavier you recognise as correction you needed.

## Allies 3 (Finance)

Your finance people have been leaning on the Mosman overtime culture all month. Late-night trading desk hours extended at two mid-tier firms, after-work drinks pushed past ten, a new cocktail bar behind Military Road that is drawing exactly the clientele you wanted. A contact sends you a photo of the bar's Tuesday crowd at 11pm. Suits on their third drink. The climb maketh the garden verdant.

## Allies 2 (Politics)

Your political people move a late-trading trial through a council subcommittee on the quiet. Three Pyrmont venues are now licensed past 2am for the duration of the pilot. A Herald business-section item the following week describes the loosening as a long-overdue modernisation. The 3am crowd arrives. The territory gets better for the kind of work you do and worse for everyone else, which is how you like it.

## Contacts

A piece of information you had not expected comes up through your networks. A vigilante has been working the criminal underside of the Dockyards across the month, beating the kind of street offender that deserves it, never to the point of killing, always to the point where the man will think seriously about his career choices. They are calling him the Tracksuit Terror. Your people have only surface-level detail, but something in the profile catches your attention for further follow-up.

OOC ST  note: We are going to roll with this, but you should not in fact have received the information that is being followed up. Our mistake, ordinarily mortal contacts do not have the ability to pierce Obfuscate - so instead, some small detail in the information combined with a small detail of Ballsack's behaviour, and Yusuf intuited the connection.

## Contacts

Your contacts work the Finance angle on the Harbour question and come back with a picture that does not point where you expected it to. Nobody in the Finance sphere is working against the territory. The serious players here are you, Xavier, and Reed, and all three of you are pulling in the same direction. Whatever is dragging the Harbour down is not coming from the money side. Your contacts do flag one detail at the edge of the enquiry: somebody matching Keeper's description has been paying for information in the Finance sector across the month, not as a player but as a buyer of intelligence about the players. File that separately.

## Contacts

Your Harbour-side contacts flag a pattern you had not been watching for. Somebody calling herself Rene has been throwing influence around in ways calculated to make the Harbour a little worse for wear. Small drags against the larger lift, targeted in places that suggest she knows what she is doing. She is working against you and Reed and Wan and everyone else who has been lifting the territory. The amount of influence is not enormous. The deliberateness of it is.

## Resources

The cult premises on the Shore receive a month's worth of investment that is designed to communicate rather than protect. New lighting, visible cameras that may or may not record, a front door that has been replaced with something heavier than a community hall requires. The interior is dressed with the quiet ostentation of money that wants to be noticed. There is no security. There does not need to be. The building looks like a trap, which is the point.

## Skill Acquisition

Three thermal cameras and two mesh monitors, encrypted, piezo-powered, the kind of equipment that does not appear in catalogues available to the public. Your police contact knows a man in the technical surveillance unit who has access to decommissioned stock. You meet him once in a carpark in Chatswood and the conversation is brief and one-directional. He leaves the equipment in the boot of a car that is not his. You collect it after he has gone. The cameras are installed on the cult premises within the week, covering every approach.

## Mystery Cult Initiate

The cult works the politics and finance arms across the month and what it produces is infrastructural. A council environmental report that quietly rules a section of the Ultimo sewer loop unsafe for maintenance access. A contractor who is awarded a small job to run a concrete seal across a junction in the M11 tunnel works, recording it in the file as a construction accident. An archived rail easement that has been un-archived and then re-archived under a different filing so that the particular section beneath the Chinese Garden now belongs, administratively, to nowhere. The total effect is that substantial stretches of the subterranean city under the Harbour now exist only in your map. Work on a Necropolis has begun.

narrative.travel_description: I can't remember the entire answer I wrote but it involved getting lost a lot. It was something like taking the wrong train and getting off at the wrong station and having to catch a train back to where I was meant to go and then still getting lost
narrative.game_recount:
Helped Anichka with braiding her red ribbons. They made her think of red ribbons of blood in the water.
Saw a fight at the club when went dancing with cyrus. Instantly went to help Kindred and keep the masquerade intact.
Tried to bargain with Conrad for regency but he just insulted all crones, then afterwards said he was only trying to shield another territory and offered a boon to Alice. She accepted it.
Became regent without much warning
Chatted with Renee about how keeper can mess with memories, then realised alice doesn't remember bits from the party with cyrus
narrative.standout_rp: Conrad, for being a religious shit. Anichka, for helping Alice realise that she is struggling with the anger of the beast.
narrative.ic_correspondence: I had an email alice sent to her family that was about the new club and how she is now in a new position and feels very nervous about it and also that all these people at the new club talking about bringing in the old ways and the old people made her feel protective of her home country
narrative.actively_harming_pc: Conrad. but not in a way where alice would endanger herself or get caught.

regency.regency_action: This is not a place to bring the old world to and impose old ways upon. This territory is its own entity, and must be respected as such.

projects.description:
Project Name: We Own the Night
Characters involved: Jack, Cyrus, Anichka, Keeper, Brandy, Alice, Charles, Ivana
Merits & Bonuses: Mantle of Amrous Fire + Viral Mythology + Cacophony Savvy
XP Spend: n/a

Project description:
Ritual Casting a variation of 'Mantle of the Predator Goddess'. 
As with Mantle of Amorous Fire, Acolytes dance themselves into a trance during the entire casting of this ritual and adopt the Blush of Life. They each must spend a point of Willpower to rise from the exhaustion. Once cast, the Acolytes flow with the grace of a perfect predator for the rest of the night. The Kine all but beg for their attention, stumbling into vulnerable positions with a subconscious prayer to prostrate themselves on her altar. The Acolytes are worshiped as Gods during the Rave. 
The vibe of this party permeates The Academy.

st_review.outcome_text:
## Letter from Home

Alice,
You buried the news about your new position three paragraphs into an email about the heating, which is exactly what you did when you got accepted into your programme and exactly what you did when you moved to Sydney in the first place. One day you will lead with the important thing, and I will need to sit down from the shock of it.
So you have a position at your club. People are apparently looking to you for something, and you are handling this by being nervous about it in writing to your family instead of telling anyone there that you are nervous about it. I recognise this because I have watched you do it your entire adult life. You collect responsibilities the way other people collect receipts, quietly and without telling anyone, and then you are surprised when the pile gets heavy.
The other thing you mentioned, about the people talking about bringing back the old ways, the old guard: I know what that sounds like to you. I know what it does to your shoulders and your jaw. You get that from Dad, who once argued with a man at a petrol station for twenty minutes about something that turned out to be a misunderstanding, and did not apologise afterwards because he said the principle still stood.
But you are there now, Alice. You chose to be there. If something needs saying, say it to the people in the room, not to me in an email at two in the morning.
Dad asks if you are eating. I am not going to pass on his exact words because they were mostly about soup.

## Touchstone

Your sister's phone buzzes three times during the walk from the car to the restaurant, and she answers none of them. This is how you know she has been looking forward to seeing you: she has decided, for the duration of the evening, that nobody else exists. She does this rarely and only for people she considers worth the silence, and you have never once taken it for granted.
She picks the place, because she always picks the place, and it is a small Vietnamese restaurant on a side street in Marrickville where the menus are laminated and the woman behind the counter remembers her order from last time. Your sister finds this deeply satisfying. She tells you about it twice: once when you sit down and once when the food arrives, as though the continuity of being remembered by a stranger is proof that the world is functioning correctly.
You order something you will not eat and move it around the plate with enough conviction that she does not notice. She is telling you about a colleague who has been promoted above his competence, and her analysis of his failings is thorough, specific, and delivered with the calm precision of someone who has been composing this speech in her head for weeks. You ask one question and she talks for another ten minutes, and the relief of being in the presence of someone who requires nothing from you except an audience is so uncomplicated that you almost forget the small rituals: the sip of water, the nod, the laugh at the right moment.
She steals a prawn off your plate without asking, which she has done since you were fourteen, and you let her, which you have done for exactly as long.

## The North Shore

The Shore is at the top of its game and you feel it every way a resident can. Military Road carries confident crowds past eleven. The restaurants in Kirribilli and the bars behind Crows Nest are running late, and foot traffic along the harbour stretches is the healthy kind. Blood comes up clean but the palate catches the faint trace of other feeders having been through ahead of you, nothing wrong with it yet, only a ledger opening. Five of your own are drawing from the territory this month, and three others are feeding here without your grant. Cyrus has been at it without asking. Ivana has been through twice. Wan Yelong is working the lecture crowd with the quiet confidence of somebody who does not think permission applies to him. Past the Oaks on a Tuesday a stranger in the queue behind you is paying closer attention than strangers usually do, and on Willoughby Road you pass through a stretch of pavement that feels listened to.

## Keeping it Tight! (Alice version, credit to Jack)

You walk the North Shore across four nights, varying your route each time. Brandy takes the harbourside paths with the slow deliberate stride of someone who wants to be noticed, and you cross her circuit twice without needing to coordinate it. Charlie is impossible to track, but notifies you that it is covering the commercial stretches around Military Road with workmanlike efficiency, and you catch the edges of Ivana's movement twice near Cremorne Point.
The territory is busy. You count at least six other Kindred feeding here over the course of the month, which is a lot of mouths for one stretch of shoreline. Wan Yelong feeds amongst the harbourside lecture crowd with the quiet confidence of someone who has been doing this for a very long time. Yusuf works the late-night bars with an intensity that leaves a mark on the atmosphere even after he has moved on. One feeder you cannot place at all; you sense the traces of their passage but never see them directly, which tells you something about their methods.
Cyrus you spot twice, feeding in the North Shore without having sought your permission. He is not hiding it.

## Happy Cult

You spend the month tending the people you have collected. A luncheon at the Mosman house on the second Sunday. A private reading group midweek. Two one-on-one conversations with members who have been anxious about the way the last few weeks have felt, and whose anxiety is useful to register rather than to dismiss. The paranoia is higher this cycle than you remember it being, and the paranoia is the bond. The cult closes around itself. Your base and your people are yours.

## We Own the Night

Your contribution is the oldest trick any of you know. Mantle of Amorous Fire layered into the ritual circle so that everyone who takes up the Cruac's invitation wears the Blush of Life for the night. You lead the portion of the chant that unlocks it. When it takes, a room full of Kindred suddenly breathes, flushes, warms at the skin, is confusable with the mortals on the dance floor below. The dancers who are paying attention take this as a miracle. The ones who are not paying attention take it as the DJ getting the drop right. Either way, the ritual cascades forward.

narrative.game_recount:
- Sat in a corner a lot and did handicrafts. Now all the Crone present (and Charlie) have ribbons from the hunt. Alice helped, so she'll be in any resonance from them, too.
- Shook hands with a lot of people, especially some of the newcomers
- Met the Bishop, that was hilarious
- Met the Keeper's LS interest, Carver. He's interesting, and only here to learn about the past. A bit like me on that one. Mentioned the old Altar. May wanna touch base about that
- Talked to Tegan ha ha she was not happy I dreamed about her.
- Did what pushing I could for Alice to guard the North Shore while Charles wasn't here. I hope she gives him a run for his money on it, I think people like having someone 'innocent' in place there, we just need to make sure she gets good guidance if she stays
- Guided plotting for next game. Made sure Jack and Cyrus (and to a lesser extent Brandi) were on it. 
- Talked to Cyrus, gave my blessing to be involved in the Hunt memories. Really wish the Keeper had been a bit more chill, he really took Cyrus's absence personally.
narrative.standout_rp: Edna/Arlo, we got about two minutes of tense pleasantries in but I've been waiting for it. Peter/Yusuf, very strong character interactions and plotting. Good fun observing the new player in the bright orange-red suit get into character. Honourable mentions to Archer and Kurtis for solid characters that appealed to Anichka.
narrative.ic_correspondence:
To my Sire,

Oh, the coven near Lviv! A very good place to remember. Are they still there? I hope they are well. I hope Halyna is well.

Sydney is... a tribulation. It is raw, cutting grass. A portion of ground has been cut out, but as always, our kind are hungry, circling, wanting more from a city that does not welcome us. Not yet.

There is so much to do. We have claimed two of the primary territories for feeding. This means I must walk the spaces to help keep them safe. My Maiden, Alice, has been put in charge of one. If anyone takes advantage of her, I think our Circle will take action. Probably sudden and violent action.

I think there is something in the ocean, making people angry. I dreamed of an opening chest there, and our littlest says she has seen an angry lady there. We will investigate, of course, but I do not know if I should put up a wanted poster saying 'Have You Seen This Lady' or not. Time will tell.

I am starting my garden. I have learned about the plants here, cut my hands on them and scratched my feet. I will make them grow and they will fruit with blood. I have also dreamed of the old temple. We will search there and find answers. But not now. We must walk the borders and let the night know what belongs to us.

I wish you could see our work, but you must stay home. When I come back, Gods willing, I will show you through all I have learned.

I remain in service,
Anichka
narrative.most_trusted_pc: Charlie, Alice, Ivana, with a hat tip to Jack
narrative.aspirations:
Short term- Secure the Academy and the North Shore for the Crone, Host good party,make sure she doesn't get eaten by whoever owns the land where she lives.
Medium term- Continued stability for the Crone, exploration and potentail restoration of the Old Temple, good Mandragora garden.
Long term- Safety and Stability of self, family, Crone, and Praxis in that order (the middle two points are close)

actions:
The family should continue living and establishing themselves, making themselves at home and part of the community, generally building their own safe space, with access to their culture for themselves and the children.

It is Orthodox Easter on the weekend of the 12th. Anichka urges her family to go if they're feeling it, especially if it will help create ties among the community and all that good shit.

She also asks them to get her a shovel, please, one for heavy duty use.

meta.st_notes:
- Anichka leaves herself open to the dreams this downtime. She is concerned about the lady who is angry that Ivana has told her about, and the blood in the water that Alice has warned her about, but does not want to force herself on the issue if The Gods do not think it is important. There might be nothing of worth this dowtime, she's chill about it. Just lights some incense sometimes, dances if she feels like it, gets a lot of good nights to sleep
- Anichka holds a little one night 'sermon' at the temple, and invites the Crone, as well as Yusuf and Charlie. My nominal concept is 'Names of the Gods;, and it's about why we do and don't need god-forces with names to worship. I'll hopefully get a wee writeup done but i'm sick as dog (as y'all know)
- After her Animal Murder feed, she does what she can to move the bodies to where she wants her Mandragora Garden to be. Burying them if she gets the chance, but just leaving them to moulder will still help.
- If she has the time, she will be participating in the ritual to get Cyrus back to his usual self, and shed the Crone's skin.
- 

- Didn't get the chance to do a Breaking Point check for the hunt last downtime
- In fact I'm not even sure she should be at Humanity 6, touchstone or nah.
- I have a massive pile of things that Anichka should have had at character creation. I'm going to be trying to buy those back as quickly as possible, but sometimes that's how the cookie crumbles. So far the list includes Crafts 2, Medicine 2, Science 1 or more (botany), Academics 1 (should probably have Subterfuge too >.>), A few dots of Primal Creation, and Shed the Virulent Bowel. Probably some merits that I can't think of right now, but here's a list of things that she should have, anyway.

I had more to say. I can't remember what it was. Maybe I'll open the form up again later tonight, but i'll submit this now in case I don't get that far.

We will search there and find answers. But not now. We must walk the borders and let the night know what belongs to us.

I wish you could see our work, but you must stay home. When I come back, Gods willing, I will show you through all I have learned.

I remain in service,
Anichka

st_review.outcome_text:
## Letter from Home

Child,
Halyna is well, and she is also teaching the spring rites to the younger ones in a way that would make you wince if you could see it. She learned from your notes rather than from mine, which I know because she makes the same mistake with the third invocation that you used to make, and I have chosen not to correct her because watching you both get it wrong in exactly the same way has become one of the small pleasures of my evenings.
The coven stands. The roof still leaks in the same corner. I mention this because you asked if they were well, and the answer is that they are exactly as they were when you left, which is the most honest measure of wellness I can offer.
Your garden will take time. The soil in that country does not know our blood yet, and it will refuse you before it accepts you, and then it will refuse you again because it has forgotten that it already accepted you. This is not a failing on your part. It is how soil works when it has not been spoken to in eighty years. Cut your hands on it as many times as necessary. The plants do not care about your comfort; they care about your persistence.
You write about the angry lady in the ocean and the old temple and the borders in the same paragraph, as though they are all one problem. They are not. You know this, because you separated them in your next sentence, but I want to be clear: walk the borders first, tend the garden second, and leave the dreaming until your feet are planted somewhere solid. The old temple has waited this long and it will wait for you.
Your Maiden Alice sounds young and recently burdened. Do not let her carry weight she has not been taught to hold. Teach her the practical things before you teach her the sacred ones, because the practical things are what will keep her standing when the sacred ones become heavy.
I am where I have always been. The roof leaks. The coven persists. Write when you can, and write longer when you do.

## Touchstone

Kateryna cannot sleep and has come to find you about it, carrying the blanket from her bed in a bundle against her chest as though it were something she had rescued. She stands in the doorway and announces this fact with the grave authority of a child who considers sleeplessness a serious injustice that someone ought to address.
You are sitting on the floor with a half-finished piece of embroidery across your knee, and you move it aside without comment to make room for her. She settles next to you, arranges the blanket into a nest around her legs, and watches your hands work the needle for a while with the particular intensity of a child who is pretending not to be tired.
She asks what you are making. You tell her it is a ribbon for a friend. She asks if the friend is nice, and you say yes, and she asks if the friend is here in Sydney, and you say yes, and she asks if Sydney is going to be home now, and you do not answer that one immediately because the question is larger than she knows.
You thread a new colour instead, a red that catches the lamplight, and she leans against your arm with the full unthinking weight of a child who has decided you are furniture. Her breathing slows. The embroidery takes shape under your hands: a pattern your grandmother taught you, carried across borders and decades and one particular kind of death, and now stitched into a ribbon in a rented house in a country where the soil does not yet know your name.
Kateryna is asleep before you finish the first repeat of the pattern. You do not move your arm.

## The Barrens

The land around Ku-ring-gai does not belong to you and it does not pretend to. You have lived here for two months now and the bush has accepted your presence the way it accepts anything that does not try to change it: with indifference and the constant quiet reminder that you are a guest in something older than your kind.
The garden is taking root slowly, and the soil resists you exactly the way your sire said it would. You have cut your hands on the local plants often enough that you have stopped noticing the sting, and the first shoots of what you planted last month have survived, which is not the same as thriving but is enough to keep going.
The strange figures you glimpsed last month on the paths toward Pittwater are still there, or others like them. You catch the edges of their movement on two occasions this month: once at dusk near the fire trail above McCarrs Creek, once on the road down to Church Point. They move through the bush with a sureness that is not human and not Kindred and not animal, and they are very good at not being where you are looking by the time you look. You have decided not to follow them yet, because the land they are moving towards does not feel like it wants you to.
The blood this month came from the rave in the Academy rather than from the bush, which was a relief your body registered even if you did not intend to acknowledge it. Animal blood sustains, but it does not satisfy, and the difference between the two is something you are learning to carry without complaint.

## Patrolling the Academy

You walk the Academy across four nights and what you come back with is the feeling more than the detail. Jack is out on the territory and you know this because the territory carries the quality of being attended to. One other patroller crosses your path near Victoria Park, a brief mutual acknowledgement and nothing more. The Beast's Hackles prickle twice on King Street where the occult shops have been busier than they should be, and once near a laneway behind Enmore Theatre where somebody has been standing for a long time without moving. You file the impressions and leave the analysis to those who patrol with more than instinct.

## Patrolling the Shore

The North Shore is less familiar ground and your patrol reads it that way, in fragments rather than in the full picture. Alice is on the territory and you feel her the way you feel any Regent walking her own ground, a settled presence that tells you the territory is already watched. Brandy you catch once on Military Road, striding with the confidence of somebody who wants to be seen. The foot traffic is healthy and late and the blood would come up clean if you were feeding here. Two faces you do not place pass you near Cremorne Point, moving with the disinterest of people who are very interested in not being interesting.

## Party Party Party

Your part of the ritual is the oldest part and the quietest. While Cyrus holds the beat and Jack seeds the crowd and Keeper works the gantry above, you move through the room at floor level with your hands open and your Cruac running low and steady. What you are doing is not visible. It sits underneath everything else. You are weaving predatory confidence into the feeding, stitching a safety net under the bacchanal so that every Crone who reaches for blood tonight reaches with certainty. The Divination in you reads the room as it tips, each feeder's moment arriving a half-beat before they know it will, and you hold the pattern until the last of them has taken what they need. Four successes. The net holds cleanly. Nobody frenzies who should not frenzy. Nobody feeds who should not feed. Your contribution is invisible and that is exactly how you prefer it.

## For the Others

You gather the honey yourself from a native bee colony in a park near the Academy, which takes the better part of a night and most of your patience. The shawl is one of your second-tier pieces, still good, still carrying the work of your hands. The pearls you add because the gesture needs weight. The kangaroo vine with fruit still on it you cut from a fence-line in Stanmore, the leaves glossy and dark. You place the offering near where you saw them last, arranged on a flat stone with the deliberateness of somebody who knows how to make an offering and who is choosing, very carefully, not to presume she knows who she is offering to. You come back twice a week across the month. The first visit, nothing has changed. The second, the honey is gone. The shawl has been moved. The pearls remain. On the third visit the vine has been replaced with a different cutting, one you did not plant, its leaves darker than any kangaroo vine you have seen in this city. Somebody has accepted the opening. Somebody has replied.

## Retainer

Your people do as they are told. A very nice shovel arrives by the end of the week, the handle wrapped in soft leather, the blade practical and sturdy. You will be putting it to use before long.
